---
pagetitle: "Projects | Alan J. Figueroa Ruiz"
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.page-hero}
# Projects

This page is the central portal to my research and outreach projects. Each major project is designed to have its own GitHub repository and project-specific website.
:::

::: {.section-block}

## Project pathways

::: {.gateway-grid}

::: {.gateway-card}
### Thesis research

Manatee Project Puerto Rico and land-sea *Toxoplasma gondii* transmission.
:::

::: {.gateway-card}
### Epidemiology

Human diagnostic records, public health interpretation, and One Health data analysis.
:::

::: {.gateway-card}
### Cell biology

Zebrafish ZF4 cells, host-pathogen interactions, and comparative infection models.
:::

::: {.gateway-card}
### Outreach platform

WildCiencia, community-based conservation, education, and future public resources.
:::

:::
:::

::: {.section-block}

## Project portfolio

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card .project-card-visual}
![Manatee Project Puerto Rico logo](images/projects/manatee-project.jpg){.project-thumb}

### Manatee Project Puerto Rico

Environmental transmission of *Toxoplasma gondii* across terrestrial, estuarine, and marine systems in Puerto Rico, with conservation relevance for the endangered Caribbean manatee.

::: {.badge-row}
[Active thesis project]{.status-badge .badge-active}
[M.S. research]{.status-badge .badge-type}
[Wildlife disease ecology]{.status-badge .badge-theme}
[Marine mammal conservation]{.status-badge .badge-theme}
[Project site coming soon]{.status-badge .badge-coming}
:::

**Project type:** Master's thesis research  
**Main focus:** Wildlife disease ecology and One Health  
**Future repository/site:** `manatee-project-puerto-rico`

[Open project website →](https://ajfigueroaruiz.github.io/manatee-project-puerto-rico/){.card-link}
:::

::: {.project-card .project-card-visual}
![ToxoREpi project identity](images/projects/human-toxo-epi.png){.project-thumb}

### Human *Toxoplasma gondii* Epidemiology in Puerto Rico

A retrospective analysis of human diagnostic records to characterize evidence of *T. gondii* exposure across Puerto Rico.

::: {.badge-row}
[Manuscript in preparation]{.status-badge .badge-manuscript}
[Epidemiology]{.status-badge .badge-type}
[Public health]{.status-badge .badge-theme}
[Reproducible R pipeline]{.status-badge .badge-theme}
[Project site coming soon]{.status-badge .badge-coming}
:::

**Project type:** Epidemiological analysis  
**Main focus:** Human diagnostic records and One Health interpretation  
**Future repository/site:** `human-toxoplasma-epidemiology-pr`

[Open project website →](https://ajfigueroaruiz.github.io/human-toxoplasma-epidemiology-pr/){.card-link}
:::

::: {.project-card .project-card-visual}
![Zebrafish ZF4 cell infection project identity](images/projects/zf4-cells.png){.project-thumb}

### Zebrafish ZF4 Cell Infection Model

A tissue and cellular biology project using zebrafish (*Danio rerio*) ZF4 cells infected with *Toxoplasma gondii*.

::: {.badge-row}
[Developing project]{.status-badge .badge-developing}
[Cell biology]{.status-badge .badge-type}
[Host-pathogen interaction]{.status-badge .badge-theme}
[Comparative model]{.status-badge .badge-theme}
[Project site coming soon]{.status-badge .badge-coming}
:::

**Project type:** Cell biology and host-pathogen interaction  
**Main focus:** Comparative infection model  
**Future repository/site:** `zf4-toxoplasma-cell-infection`

[Open project website →](https://ajfigueroaruiz.github.io/zf4-toxoplasma-cell-infection/){.card-link}
:::

::: {.project-card .project-card-visual}
![WildCiencia logo](images/projects/wildciencia.png){.project-thumb}

### WildCiencia

A science communication and outreach initiative focused on wildlife, conservation, One Health, and Puerto Rican island science.

::: {.badge-row}
[Developing outreach platform]{.status-badge .badge-outreach}
[Science communication]{.status-badge .badge-type}
[Community-based conservation]{.status-badge .badge-theme}
[Education]{.status-badge .badge-theme}
[Platform coming soon]{.status-badge .badge-coming}
:::

**Project type:** Outreach and science communication  
**Main focus:** Community-based conservation education  
**Future repository/site:** `wildciencia`

[Open project website →](https://ajfigueroaruiz.github.io/wildciencia/){.card-link}
:::

:::
:::

::: {.section-block}

## Project status guide

::: {.badge-legend}

::: {.badge-legend-item}
[Active thesis project]{.status-badge .badge-active}  
Currently central to my graduate research.
:::

::: {.badge-legend-item}
[Manuscript in preparation]{.status-badge .badge-manuscript}  
Research output currently being developed for publication.
:::

::: {.badge-legend-item}
[Developing project]{.status-badge .badge-developing}  
Project direction, content, or repository structure is being built.
:::

::: {.badge-legend-item}
[Developing outreach platform]{.status-badge .badge-outreach}  
Public-facing education, outreach, or communication platform in development.
:::

:::
:::

::: {.section-block}

## Portal ecosystem

::: {.expedition-grid}

::: {.expedition-card}
### Main portal

Professional identity, CV, research overview, teaching, outreach, publications, and contact.
:::

::: {.expedition-card}
### Project websites

Dedicated websites for each major research and outreach project.
:::

::: {.expedition-card}
### Repositories

Code, scripts, documentation, figures, protocols, and reproducible workflows.
:::

::: {.expedition-card}
### Future platforms

WildCiencia, educational products, public resources, and possible shop integration.
:::

:::
:::

::: {.section-block}

## Repository strategy

The main website functions as my professional academic portal. Each major project will live in its own GitHub repository with its own Quarto website, allowing each project to grow independently while keeping a consistent visual identity.

::: {.note-box}
**Main portal:** `ajfigueroaruiz.github.io`  
**Project websites:** separate repositories published with GitHub Pages.  
**Future expansion:** project sites, outreach platforms, educational resources, and potential shop pages can reuse this design system.
:::

:::
